hero_product_name: the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS electric wheelchair from the white-background reference photos
campaign_goal: Create a stronger moving-route version of the approved AnyWell creative, with real forward travel, light terrain texture, and smoother scene-to-scene flow.
target_audience: Clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size older adults, their spouses, and adult children buying mobility support for loved ones.
core_selling_points:
- 22-inch wide seat
- 330 lb capacity
- smooth 500W power
- FDA-cleared safety
- 30-day trial and easy returns
- PayPal financing
use_scenarios:
- home threshold to stone path
- compacted gravel park path
- wooded park trail with light natural texture
visual_style: Warm, realistic, cinematic, portrait-first Western lifestyle advertising with clear route progression, visible forward motion, and restrained emotional tone.
style_tone: warm, calm, credible, dignified, cinematic, never melodramatic
consistency_anchor: Match the real YS16-1 PLUS wheelchair from the white-background product photos in its normal open riding position only. Preserve the right-side joystick, wide seat feel, armrests, front caster, large drive wheel profile, tubular frame, footrest shape, and a compact top-back profile. Do not generate tall rear caregiver handles, upright rods, cane-like poles, squeeze levers, or any long parts sticking up above the backrest. If tiny integrated rear handles are unavoidable, keep them short, low, close to the backrest, and visually secondary. Do not show any rear/lower external battery pack, cable, folded form, semi-folded form, storage form, or rear-facing angle.
additional_info: The last approved packaging for voiceover and subtitles is good; this pass is about improving the moving image only. The rider must visibly travel through space, not appear to hover or drift in place. Every shot should show real forward progress through the environment with background parallax, changing landmarks, and visible wheel travel over the ground. The route should feel continuous and believable: start at the home threshold, continue onto a compacted stone or gravel garden path, then move into a park or wooded trail with light natural obstacles such as shallow gravel texture, small scattered stones, gentle undulation, compacted dirt patches, leaf litter, or soft root ripples. Keep the terrain realistic and safe, never extreme. Motion must stay left to right across all three scenes. Camera movement should track with the wheelchair naturally so the cuts feel like one outing, not three separate demos.
music: Warm restrained cinematic underscore with soft ambient texture.

concepts.title: Dynamic Route Outdoor Proof
concepts.core_theme: The same rider steadily advances from home into a more textured outdoor route, with real motion and believable terrain.
concepts.main_theme: Freedom should feel steady and close to home.
concepts.cta: See how AnyWell helps bring the outdoors back within reach.
concepts.visual_style: Continuous left-to-right movement, compacted stone paths, park greenery, wooded trail texture, and natural camera tracking.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the exact same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired older man and the exact same wife across all three shots. Preserve his broad torso and shoulders, rounded belly, thicker arms and legs, heavier seated posture, silver hair, clean-shaven soft face, charcoal knit jacket, muted blue shirt, dark trousers, and joystick-side orientation consistently. Keep the same compact top-back silhouette too: no tall rear poles, no caregiver handles, and no squeeze levers protruding above the backrest.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- doorway to stone garden path
- compacted gravel park path
- wooded park trail with light obstacles
concepts.additional_info: This must feel like one continuous outing with obvious scene progression. Scene 1 starts at the threshold and clearly exits onto a stone or compacted gravel path. Scene 2 is deeper into a park-like route with visible wheel movement over compacted gravel, scattered small stones, and gentle path texture. Scene 3 continues onto a slightly more natural wooded trail with shallow undulation, leaf litter, compacted dirt, or subtle root texture while staying safe and believable. Keep the rider independently driving with the right hand visibly on the joystick in every moving shot. Preserve left-to-right route logic and use transition anchors that let one shot flow naturally into the next.

shots.scene_description: The same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired American man in his 60s sits in the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS at a quiet back doorway threshold, visibly filling the seat with his broad torso, rounded belly, thicker arms and legs, and heavier seated posture. He wears a charcoal knit zip jacket over a muted blue shirt with dark trousers. His right hand rests clearly on the right-side joystick controller as he independently drives left to right out of the doorway and onto a compacted stone-and-gravel garden path. The same wife in a cream cardigan and muted blue top follows a half-step behind with warm relaxed body language, never touching the chair. The camera stays on the joystick-side front profile so the viewer sees his front torso, soft side profile, right forearm, joystick hand, front caster, armrest, and side wheel profile. The top-back area stays compact and unobtrusive behind him, with no tall rods, caregiver handles, or lever details rising above the backrest. The chair must visibly move forward through space, with the doorway receding behind him and the front caster clearly rolling onto the textured stone path. The back panel and lower rear quadrant remain hidden.
shots.camera_movement: smooth joystick-side tracking move that begins near the doorway and glides forward with the chair as it rolls onto the compacted stone path, keeping real background drift and forward travel visible
shots.lighting: soft morning daylight with natural highlights on the path texture, chair frame, and surrounding greenery
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide front-side composition with the doorway clearly behind him, the stone-and-gravel garden path visible ahead, and enough parallax to make the forward movement unmistakable
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair already committed to the stone path, still moving left to right toward a wider park-like walkway beyond the yard
shots.voiceover_en: At the doorway, freedom begins in his own hand. The 22-inch seat feels calm and secure.
shots.key_message: Independent driving and wide-seat comfort are clear from the first shot.
shots.sfx: soft wheel roll on stone, distant birds, light morning ambience

shots.scene_description: The exact same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired man continues along a compacted gravel park path, still moving left to right in the same wheelchair with his right hand clearly on the joystick and independently controlling the chair. His body type, clothing, posture, silver hair, clean-shaven soft face, and seat-filling silhouette remain obviously identical to scene one. The same wife walks to the side at the same pace without touching the chair. The path now has more visible texture: compacted gravel, scattered small stones, light surface undulation, and a gentle crown that the wheelchair rolls over smoothly and realistically. The camera tracks from the joystick-side front profile so his front torso, soft facial profile, right forearm, joystick hand, armrest, and side wheel profile remain readable. The top-back area stays compact and unobtrusive behind him, with no tall rods, caregiver handles, or lever details rising above the backrest. The background should clearly slide past, showing trees, park lawn, and path markers receding so the chair feels genuinely in motion rather than stationary.
shots.camera_movement: steady joystick-side front-profile tracking shot matching the chair speed, with visible foreground and background parallax to emphasize forward travel over the gravel path
shots.lighting: clean daylight through trees with realistic contrast, soft shadows, and enough side light to reveal the texture of the gravel and wheel movement
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium shot with the gravel path stretching behind and ahead, the rider and chair occupying most of the frame height, park greenery and open space sliding by in the background
shots.transition_anchor: end as the path curves gently toward a more wooded section where the gravel blends into packed earth and light leaf cover, still moving left to right
shots.voiceover_en: On the path, 500 watts of smooth power and 330-pound support keep the ride steady.
shots.key_message: Power and steady control read clearly in the middle shot.
shots.sfx: soft gravel roll, leaves moving lightly, open park ambience

shots.scene_description: The exact same clearly obese, heavyset, plus-size silver-haired man and the same wife continue into a wooded park trail with more natural texture, while still staying safe and believable. He remains clearly the same rider with the same charcoal jacket, muted blue shirt, dark trousers, silver hair, clean-shaven soft face, broad torso, rounded belly, thicker arms and legs, and heavier seat-filling posture. He continues moving left to right with his right hand visible on the joystick while the wife walks beside him at the same pace without pushing. The trail should show light natural obstacles only: compacted dirt mixed with fine gravel, shallow leaf litter, subtle root ripples, and gentle unevenness that the wheelchair handles smoothly. The camera stays front-side on the joystick side, showing his chest or soft side profile, right forearm, joystick hand, and front-side wheelchair profile. The top-back area stays compact and unobtrusive behind him, with no tall rods, caregiver handles, or lever details rising above the backrest. Never show his back or the lower rear quadrant. The shot should feel like real forward travel into a calmer open patch of the trail, not a parked hero pose.
shots.camera_movement: gentle front-side tracking move that follows the chair along the wooded trail, then eases into a natural settling move without losing the feeling of forward progress
shots.lighting: clean daylight with filtered tree light, realistic depth, and soft highlights on the moving chair and path surface
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait hero composition with wooded trail texture in the foreground, open space and trees ahead, and enough movement in the environment to keep the shot feeling alive and continuous
shots.transition_anchor: end on a smooth continuing glide suitable for a calm branded close, with the route still feeling open ahead
shots.voiceover_en: When the family is ready, AnyWell adds FDA-cleared confidence, easy returns, and PayPal financing.
shots.subtitle_text: FDA-cleared. 30-day trial. Easy returns. PayPal financing.
shots.key_message: The close lands on trust signals in short readable phrases.
shots.sfx: light trail ambience, soft breeze, distant birds
